verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically cytosolic but translocates to the plasma membrane of stressed and cancer cells via direct interaction with phosphatidylserine and phosphoinositides, anchoring itself to the outer leaflet. Cell-surface biotinylation confirms exposure to the extracellular face during heat shock and in cancer cells. This is a well-documented, condition-dependent surface display (stress-induced and cancer-cell-restricted) achieved through the protein's own lipid-binding mechanism, not transient recruitment.
